legal_compliance_checklist.description: Obtain authorization for installing IoT-enabled parcel lockers in public spaces, especially in university/apartment zones.
Ensure customer geolocation data and IoT device logs comply with Tunisia's data protection laws.
Verify hardware meets Tunisian technical standards for connected devices and cybersecurity requirements.
Define clear terms for liability in case of locker malfunctions, lost packages, or delivery errors in B2B2C agreements.
Comply with Tunisia's e-commerce laws for subscription-based services and digital transaction records.

trust_requirements.description: Demonstrate physical and digital security measures to protect customer packages and personal data.
Clearly disclose subscription fees, usage-based charges, and any hidden costs for merchants and customers.
Provide customers with reliable access to parcel location data via the locker network.
Establish SLAs for handling delivery failures, locker malfunctions, or data breaches.

special_operational_constraints.description: Adhere to local ordinances for deploying physical infrastructure in dense urban areas.
Comply with Tunisia's data storage laws, which may require local server hosting for user data.
Ensure hardware compatibility with local telecom networks and payment systems.
Structure agreements with couriers and merchants to clarify responsibilities for lost packages or service disruptions.
